fingerprint: The dome's 94.5% accuracy IS the globe's accuracy rate. 70% of WINs explicitly relabel standard physics predictions; 97% are post-hoc retrodictions of known observations; only 2 of 70 derive from dome geometry (both rated Misleading). The accuracy metric measures how well globe physics predicts globe-observed phenomena — the dome contributes nothing to the numerator.
globe_value: Standard physics prediction accuracy for 49 known phenomena (Schumann resonance, Chapman eclipse mechanism, tidal periodicity, WGS84 gravity, Kepler orbital mechanics, etc.)
match_precision: 70% exact relabeling (49/70 WINs), 97% post-hoc (68/70), 97% no dome derivation (68/70)
why_it_matters: The entire accuracy statistic is a globe fingerprint. A flat-disc cosmology that independently derived predictions should have a mix of novel and standard-physics confirmations. Instead, nearly every confirmation is a pre-existing globe prediction with the mechanism renamed from 'ionospheric/gravitational/orbital' to 'aetheric.' The 2 WINs that DO derive from dome geometry (WIN-044 FSF, WIN-057 two-zone topology) are both rated Misleading — neither qualifies as a genuine confirmed prediction. If the dome's accuracy were computed only over predictions that actually derive from dome physics, the numerator would be 0.
section_already_notes: Section 6.6 mentions 'non-discriminating predictions,' 're-sliced data,' and 'globe values relabeled' qualitatively but does not quantify the fingerprint. The TLDR says 'standard physics already explains' but the body text lacks the precise code_analysis statistics that make the case devastating: 70% relabels, 97% post-hoc, 97% no dome derivation, 0 genuine dome-derived confirmations.
suggested_text: One additional paragraph for Section 6.6: 'Our code-by-code analysis of the model's source repository quantifies this precisely. Of the dome's reviewed WINs, 70% explicitly relabel standard physics mechanisms as "aetheric" — the observation is real, but the explanation is not novel. 97% are post-hoc: the observation was published in peer-reviewed literature before the dome "predicted" it. Most tellingly, 97% do not derive from dome geometry at all — no formula in the repository connects dome parameters (disc radius, firmament height, aetheric index) to the predicted value. Only 2 WINs have any derivation from dome geometry, and both are rated Misleading in our review. If the accuracy were computed only over predictions that actually originate from dome physics, the numerator would be zero.'

code_search.found_in: docs/model.html: '69 confirmed / (69 + 4) = 94.5%' as static HTML string
code_search.notes: The accuracy figure is manually entered. scorecard.json tracks per-category counts (34+2+26+1=63) but does not compute an overall percentage. results.json has 35 entries (32 with null data, auto-confirmed). No script in the entire repository computes or validates the headline accuracy.
